E

Labcyte Echo

Echo 550 ┬и Cherry Pick ┬и Dose Response ┬и Data Analysis

Dark mode
Labcyte Echo 550
Acoustic liquid handler ┬и 2.5 nL resolution ┬и No tips, no contact ┬и DMSO and aqueous liquid classes
Software Modules
Cherry Pick
Transfer specific volumes from defined source wells to destination wells. The standard mode for compound dispensing. Input: picklist CSV (Source Plate, Source Well, Destination Plate, Destination Well, Transfer Volume).
Beckman Echo РєЌ
Dose Response Standard
Automated serial dilution dispensing. Routes compounds through intermediate plates and performs DMSO backfill to equalise DMSO % across all wells. Input: concentration series design. Use the Gradient Planner tab to design your series.
Plate Reformat
Copy and reformat plate layouts across different formats (e.g. 96-well Рєњ 384-well). Handles volume scaling and well mapping automatically. Useful for reformatting library plates or combining assay conditions.
Survey
Non-destructively measures the volume and fluid height in each well of a source plate using acoustic survey. Identifies low-volume or empty wells before a dispensing run, preventing failed transfers.
Normalisation
Dispenses variable volumes from a source plate to bring all compounds in a destination plate to the same concentration. Uses survey data and stock concentrations to calculate exact transfer volumes per well.
Worklist
Import a custom worklist CSV to chain multiple transfer operations in a single run. Allows complex multi-step protocols Рђћ e.g. compound dispensing followed by DMSO backfill Рђћ in one automated sequence.

Echo Data Analysis Рђћ User Guide
4PL curve fitting for HiBiT degradation assays ┬и Echo liquid handler + PHERAstar plate reader

­ЪЊЂ Input files

Echo CSV
Transfer file from the Echo liquid handler. Required columns: Sample ID, Destination Plate Barcode, Destination Well, Destination Concentration (in M). The barcode links each transfer well to the matching PHERAstar plate.
PHERAstar XLS / XLSX
One file per assay plate. Filename (without extension) must exactly match the plate barcode in the Echo CSV. Luminescence data is read from rows 16РђЊ32, columns 2РђЊ26 (standard 384-well HiBiT layout).
SMILES CSV (optional)
Two-column file with Sample ID and SMILES headers. Enables structure display and computed ADMET/PK properties in the Properties tab.
РџЌ Load test data
Loads a built-in set of example files (Echo CSV + 6 PHERAstar plates) so you can explore the app without uploading real data. Useful for testing or demos.

РџЎ№ИЈ Parameters

Assay ID
Free-text label used to name output files: Results_ID_Group.pdf. Use a date or experiment code. Leave blank for a generic name.
Control wells range
DMSO-only wells used as 100% reference. Signals are normalised per plate to the mean of these wells. Accepted formats: B12-O12 (column range), B22:O23 (rectangular block), B12,C12 (comma-separated). Leave blank to use raw luminescence without normalisation.
Echo header rows
Metadata rows above the column header in the Echo CSV. Default: 8 for standard Echo Plate Reformat output.
Hook effect threshold (%)
A hook effect occurs when HiBiT signal rises at the highest concentrations Рђћ typically from ternary complex disruption. If the mean signal at the highest concentration exceeds the mean at the 3rd-highest by more than this threshold, that point is flagged (red ├Ќ) and excluded from fitting. Default: 10%.
Min R┬▓ for fit
Minimum acceptable R┬▓ for a 4PL curve. Compounds below this are reported but flagged in red. Default: 0.8.
Max replicate SD
Maximum SD (in % DMSO) across replicates at any concentration. Compounds exceeding this are flagged as noisy. Default: 20.

­ЪЊі Output values

DC50 (nM)
Concentration achieving 50% of maximal degradation Рђћ derived from the EC50 of the 4PL fit, converted to nM.
Dmax (%)
Maximum degradation at plateau = 100% Рѕњ bottom asymptote of the 4PL fit. A Dmax of 80% means 20% of target remains at saturating compound.
LogIC50 (M)
LogРѓЂРѓђ of the inflection point (EC50 in molar units) from the 4PL fit. Useful for comparing potency across orders of magnitude on the scatter plot X-axis.
Hill slope
Cooperativity / steepness of the sigmoidal curve. Values near 1 are typical; values >2 may indicate steep cooperativity or data artefacts.
R┬▓
Goodness of fit for the 4PL curve. Values below the Min R┬▓ threshold are highlighted in red in the results table.
PDF reports
One PDF per experimental group, one compound per page. Each page shows mean ┬▒ SD across replicates, 4PL fit line, DC50 / Dmax / R┬▓ stats box, hook-excluded points (├Ќ), and any QC flags.
CSV summary
Consolidated table with all fitted parameters for every compound. Includes Flag and Flag_Reason columns for full QC traceability.

­Ъћг Scatter & Selectivity plots

Plot type
Three modes: Scatter (any X vs Y parameter), Box plot (distribution per group), and Selectivity (Protein A vs Protein B for the same compound Рђћ see below).
X / Y axis
Choose any calculated parameter for each axis. Default view: X = LogIC50 (M), Y = Dmax (%) Рђћ potency vs. efficacy.
X / Y min / max
Number inputs to set axis limits manually. Leave blank for automatic scaling. Clear a value to revert to auto.
­ЪћЇ Search
Type a partial compound ID. Matching points highlight in yellow; non-matching points fade. A live match counter is shown next to the input.
Names
Toggle compound ID labels on data points. Labels use a text outline (no background box) to minimise overlap with neighbouring points.
Group
Draw a convex hull around each experimental group, colour-coded per group, for visual clustering.
Dot size
Slider to adjust the point radius.
PNG
Export a high-resolution PNG (1200 DPI equivalent). All active overlays (labels, hulls, reference lines) are included in the export.

­ЪЊд Box plot

Y axis (metric)
Select which calculated parameter to display: DC50 (nM), Dmax (%), LogIC50 (M), Hill slope, or R┬▓. Each compound contributes one box per group.
X axis (grouping)
Currently fixed to Group Рђћ compounds are grouped by their experimental group label (protein / target). Future versions may support custom grouping variables.
Group filter
Show all groups or restrict to a single group for a cleaner view when many targets are loaded.
Flagged
Toggle to include or exclude compounds that failed QC (low R┬▓, noisy replicates, hook-only fits).
Y min / max
Set manual axis limits for the box plot Y-axis. Leave blank for automatic scaling.
PNG
Exports the current box plot as a PNG image.

­Ъј» Selectivity plot

What it shows
Plots Protein A value vs Protein B value for each compound shared between both datasets. Points on the diagonal (y=x) have equal activity on both targets; points above are selective for the Y protein; points below are selective for X.
Protein X / Y
Select which two experimental groups to compare. Populated automatically from your loaded data.
Metric
Any fitted parameter: pDC50, DC50 nM (log scale), Dmax %, Hill slope, R┬▓. For log-scale metrics, the fold selectivity is computed as 10^|yРѕњx|.
Selectivity bands
Coloured diagonal bands at ┬▒N log units from the y=x line. Green = selective for Y protein, red = selective for X protein, grey = within threshold. Band threshold is configurable (10├Ќ, 100├Ќ, ~3├Ќ).
Only shared compounds
When checked, only compounds with data in both Protein X and Protein Y are shown. Uncheck to also display compounds present in only one group (plotted at the axis edge).

­ЪЊі Scatter tools

РќА Select
Draw a rectangle on the scatter plot to select compounds. Copy their Sample IDs to clipboard with ­ЪЊІ Copy IDs. Press Escape or click Select again to cancel.
РгЄ CSV (filtered)
Exports the currently visible (filtered) scatter data as a CSV file including all fitted parameters.
Undo / Redo filters
Each time you change a filter (group, axis range, search), the state is saved. Use Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y to step back and forward through filter history.
Curve on hover
When checked, hovering over a scatter point shows a mini 4PL curve preview for that compound in a floating panel.

­Ъћг Plate view

Signal intensity
Colours each well by its normalised luminescence (%) using a viridis colour scale. Controls appear as green-tinted wells.
Raw signal QC
New. Colours wells by their robust z-score relative to the plate median (MAD-based). Blue = unusually low signal (possible empty well / poor cell dispensing); red = unusually high (double dispensing or background artefact). Ideal for catching dispensing errors before interpreting results.
DC50 / Dmax (fitted)
Colours each well by the fitted DC50 or Dmax of the compound at that position. Only populated after curve fitting.

­ЪДф Properties tab

Sortable columns
Click any column header to sort by that property (ascending Рєњ descending Рєњ reset). An arrow indicator (Рќ▓Рќ╝) shows the active sort column and direction.
SMILES
The SMILES cell shows a truncated string. Hover to see the full SMILES in a tooltip. Click to copy the full SMILES string to the clipboard.
Ro5
Lipinski Rule of Five: flags compounds with MW РЅЦ 500, logP РЅЦ 5, HBA > 10, or HBD > 5. Shows number of violations.

­ЪњА Tips

РЊў icons
Hover over any i icon next to a parameter label for a quick explanation of what it controls and how to set it.
Keyboard shortcuts
In the Curves tab: Рєљ / Рєњ to navigate between compounds; scroll to zoom; drag to pan; double-click to reset zoom. Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y to undo/redo scatter filters.
Progress indicator
A small bat ­ЪдЄ appears in the bottom-right corner during analysis, flapping to show the app is running. The progress bar shows N / Total curves fitted ┬и X% in real time.
